name: Storytime
description: Selfie talking-head narrative — creator looks straight into camera and tells a personal story (dating fiasco, family drama, work incident, true crime, throwback). 60-180s — one of the few long formats that works on short-form feeds. The story format breaks scroll rules: viewers commit because they want the payoff. Strong pre-hook in the first 2s, escalating tension with pattern-interrupts every 8-15s (b-roll cutaway, screenshot insert, gesture zoom), then a payoff or cliffhanger that drives DM-share and part-2 follow. Subtitles mandatory — 85% of viewers watch muted. Distinct from talking-head-rant (contrarian opinion) and yap-talking-head (single education idea): storytime is plot-driven and rides on rising tension, not on a hot take.

stackSummary.characterKeyframe: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — one photoreal selfie keyframe of the narrator (front camera framing, natural light, conversational warmth in the eyes). The same keyframe anchors every talking-head clip so the character stays consistent across a 60-180s runtime.
stackSummary.screenshotInserts: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — pattern-interrupt assets: text-message screenshots, Reddit posts, fake news headlines, frozen-frame thought-bubbles. 2-5 inserts per video at the tension turns.
stackSummary.talkingHead: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, i2v from the character keyframe, 5-10s clips stitched into the narration spine. generate_audio: false — VO is added separately so we can re-cut without re-rendering. Optional veo-3.1 swap for the pre-hook clip when premium lip-sync matters.
stackSummary.broll: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter — 3-7 short cutaway clips (5s each) for pattern-interrupts: hand gestures, environment beats, prop close-ups, reenactment moments. generate_audio: false.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — long-form narration with stability ~0.35 for natural emotional swings (anger, surprise, embarrassment, relief). Sliced into beat-aligned chunks so VO timing matches each pattern-interrupt cut.
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1 → Caption[] consumed by HormoziCaptions (default — high impact for tension peaks) or TikTok-white-stroke caption style. Word-level timing is mandatory; 85% of viewers watch muted.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — single ambient/tense bed under the entire story, force_instrumental: true, ducked to -12dB during VO peaks and lifted +3dB at the payoff frame. Optional cliffhanger sting at the final beat.

constants.structure: 0-2s pre-hook → 2-10s setup → 10-60s rising tension w/ interrupts → 60-90s payoff or cliffhanger

variationAxes: story type (dating-fiasco / family-drama / work-incident / school-throwback / true-crime)
narrator archetype (Gen-Z urban / millennial professional / stay-at-home parent / older-confidant)
VO language (any — English default; the format is narrative-driven, not language-specific)
doNotCopyLiterally: Story content, narrator name, exact pattern-interrupt visuals, the literal pre-hook line. Vibe and structure only.
referenceNotes: No reference required by default — the narrator is generated. If the user wants a specific real person (e.g. their own face, a named creator), escalate to the reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) and require an uploaded photo. Without a reference, never claim the story belongs to a real named person — it must read as a generic first-person narrator.
